---
title: "ReBioClim analytical workflow collaboration notebook"
format: html
---


# prepare stream geometry (without segments)
- all osm waterway
    - from [`osm_stream_extract.py`](scripts/osm_stream_extract.py), osm way+relation, both extracted, for all possible tags with stream, much more complete than directly use the waterway=stream data from geofabrick or osmmx
    - dissolve the stream where name is the same
    - also add drain, ditch, culvert, river, for complete waterway lines as much as possible
    - output is [`data/stream_geometry/allblue.gpkg`](data/stream_geometry/allblue.gpkg)
- clean stream geometry
    - [`clean_stream_geometry.py`](scripts/clean_stream_geometry.py)    
    - from now on, the stream we use is the above [`allblue.gpkg`](data/stream_geometry/allblue.gpkg) and with its river removed
    - remove some of "drain" "ditch" when it is not connected to any other waterway or allblue polygon (remove unnecessary stream endings)
    - dissolve the ones with same name
    - merge to continuous lines
    - only keep the lines >=100m (remove the standalone short streams, since it is 100m, it is not considered in our analysis)
- Dresden watercourse data replaced (provided by Dresden-municipality-Anna)
    - [`clean_stream_geometry.py`](scripts/clean_stream_geometry.py)  
    - the output is [`data/stream_geometry/streamall.gpkg`](data/stream_geometry/streamall.gpkg)

# create segments
- 100m segment from WFD? cannot find the datasource for the 100m segmentation. 
    the WFD related values only vary when there is a sub-stream
- create 100m segments in [`create_stream_segments.py`](scripts/create_stream_segments.py). 
    - create points with 100m intervals
        - did not specifically identify the mouth of stream and start from there; sometimes the mouth is a waterbody, so there is always some missing, so I doubt the value of starting from mouth
    - create segments with snap to the point
- add original attribute
- only keep the ones with length between [99-101]m
    - when cutting the lines into 100m, the results show there are some minor variations, so 99 to 101 m is still acceptable
- remove overlaps
- output is [`data/stream_geometry/streamall_segment100.gpkg`](data/stream_geometry/streamall_segment100.gpkg), n=5745

# calculate variable
## variable 1 impervious cover
